mechanism_narrative: High and rising inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real returns on equities and prompts tighter monetary policy from the central bank, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate margins are squeezed and discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 5%, or the Rank IC is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as economic activity contracts.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over the preceding 3 months.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, or the signal fails to predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining real retail sales (RSAFS) and falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based weakening in consumer demand and investment, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth is negative AND HOUST 3-month average growth is negative.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show negative 3-month average growth, or the interaction effect is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of aggressive monetary tightening, a rapid increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the cost of capital and reduces liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as discount rates rise and economic activity slows.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.75 percentage points in a single month.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 30% of periods following a 75bp+ FEDFUNDS hike during an aggressive tightening cycle, or the signal fails to predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained weakness in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader supply-side constraints, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO shows a negative 3-month average growth, or the Rank IC is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a certain threshold indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the signal fails to predict positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust job market and healthy consumer base, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive 6-month average growth, or the Rank IC is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, if the Federal Reserve continues to raise interest rates (FEDFUNDS) while GDP growth (GDPC1) is already slowing, it signals an increased risk of recession and stagflation, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investor confidence erodes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased by > 1.0% over 6 months AND CPIAUCSL is > 5% YoY AND GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth is < 1.0%.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where all trigger conditions are met, or the Rank IC is not significantly negative over any 2-year rolling window.
